original_title: Facilities Management & Workplace Services DPS
english_summary: This is a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for facilities management and workplace services, intended as an alternative procurement route for lower value and less complex contracts. It covers maintenance, repairs, installation works, CAFM, help-desk services, and waste management for public sector estates.
what_they_want: Facilities management and workplace services including maintenance, response repairs, supply and installation works, CAFM (Computer-Aided Facilities Management), help-desk services, and waste management services. No specific medical devices, equipment, model numbers or brands mentioned.
eligibility_requirements: Open to all suppliers with particular aim to attract SME suppliers. Available to Central Government Departments and all UK Public Sector Bodies including Local Authorities, Health, Police, Fire and Rescue, Education, Housing, Charities, Not for Profit, Nuclear, Devolved Administrations and International organizations.

original_title: Contract Notice
english_summary: The UK Health Security Agency is establishing a 4-year Dynamic Purchasing System (2024-2028) for COVID-19 vaccines to support future immunisation campaigns. The DPS allows qualified suppliers to participate in future tender opportunities with a potential total aggregate value of £1 billion, though no minimum volume commitment is guaranteed.
what_they_want: COVID-19 vaccines and product(s) indicated for protection from COVID-19 for immunisation campaigns.
eligibility_requirements: Suppliers must submit and have their DPS application approved through the Atamis eTendering system before being eligible to participate in future tenders. Only suppliers accepted onto the DPS at the time an ITT (Invitation to Tender) is issued will be eligible to submit bids. Specific qualifying criteria are available in the Atamis system documentation.

original_title: Child and Adolescent Mental Health Services (CAMHS) Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS)
buyer_name: North Staffordshire Combined Healthcare NHS Trust
english_summary: North Staffordshire Combined Healthcare NHS Trust is reopening a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for Child and Adolescent Mental Health Services (CAMHS) covering North Staffordshire and Stoke on Trent. The DPS, which went live in February 2023 for a 5-year period, allows qualified organizations to bid on future CAMHS service requirements offering face-to-face, digital, and blended delivery approaches.
what_they_want: Child and Adolescent Mental Health Services (CAMHS) for 0-25 age spectrum (primarily 0-18 years). Services must include multi-disciplinary team approaches, strengths-based and trauma-informed care with outcome focus, use of appropriate outcome measure tools, and deliver timely, high quality accessible services for children, young people and their families/carers. Delivery modalities include face-to-face, digital, and blended approaches.
eligibility_requirements: Suitably experienced organisations with expertise in Child and Adolescent Mental Health Services. Applicants must indicate their area(s) of expertise and delivery modalities as part of the qualification process. Must register via MLCSU Atamis eTendering System.

original_title: CHC Nursing Home Beds Preferred Provider List- NHS BLMK ICB
buyer_name: NHS Bedfordshire, Luton & Milton Keynes Integrated Care Board
english_summary: NHS Bedfordshire, Luton and Milton Keynes Integrated Care Board is seeking qualified providers of Continuing Healthcare (CHC) nursing home beds to deliver safe and appropriate care in registered nursing home settings across the BLMK population. This is an Any Qualified Provider framework with zero value/volume contracts for 5 years with optional 2-year extension.
what_they_want: Continuing Healthcare (CHC) nursing home beds services to be delivered in registered nursing home settings across Bedfordshire, Luton and Milton Keynes.
eligibility_requirements: Suitably qualified providers of CHC nursing home beds services; must be formal registered nursing home providers.

original_title: CHC Nursing Home Beds Preferred Provider List- NHS Bedfordshire, Luton and Milton Keynes ICB
english_summary: NHS Bedfordshire, Luton and Milton Keynes ICB is seeking qualified providers of Continuing Healthcare (CHC) nursing home beds to deliver safe and appropriate care in registered nursing home settings across their population. This is an Any Qualified Provider (AQP) contract on a zero value, zero volume basis for up to 7 years.
what_they_want: Continuing Healthcare (CHC) nursing home beds services to be delivered in registered nursing home settings across Bedfordshire, Luton and Milton Keynes.
eligibility_requirements: Suitably qualified providers of CHC nursing home beds services; must have formal registered nursing home status.

original_title: Mental Health Recovery
english_summary: Rotherham Metropolitan Borough Council is seeking experienced providers to deliver community mental health care and support services through a Flexible Purchasing System (FPS). The services will focus on mental health recovery, personalized social care, and supporting independent living for people with mental ill-health in Rotherham.
what_they_want: Community mental health care and support services including supported living accommodation-based services and day opportunities, all based on mental health recovery principles. NOT medical devices or equipment.
eligibility_requirements: Experienced providers with the capability and capacity to deliver community mental health care and support. Applicants must register with the Social Value Portal and commit to delivering Social Value additionality via all contracts awarded.

original_title: DPS 4 - Supply of community equipment for small moving & handling equipment
english_summary: Croydon Council is procuring suppliers for a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) to provide community equipment for multiple local authorities through an Integrated Procurement Hub. This specific notice is for DPS 4, which covers 32 categories of small moving and handling equipment.
what_they_want: Small moving and handling equipment across 32 categories of community equipment. No specific model numbers or brands mentioned.
eligibility_requirements: Suppliers must meet DPS admission criteria set out in the Pre-Qualification Questionnaire (PQQ). Unsuccessful suppliers may re-submit revised PQQ at any time. The DPS is open to new suppliers throughout the validity period.

notice_id: Croydon Integrated Procurement Hub - DN179859 - DPS3
original_title: DPS 3 - Supply of community equipment for Simple Aids
english_summary: Croydon Council is establishing a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for the supply of simple aids community equipment on behalf of itself and partner local authorities through the Integrated Procurement Hub. This is DPS 3 covering 102 categories of simple aids, operating as part of a shared procurement arrangement for community equipment including items required for delegated NHS health functions.
what_they_want: Simple aids community equipment across 102 categories. Specific devices, model numbers, or brands are not mentioned in this notice.
eligibility_requirements: Suppliers must meet the DPS admission criteria set out in the Pre-Qualification Questionnaire (PQQ). Unsuccessful suppliers may re-submit revised PQQs at any time. Specific certifications, experience levels, or company size requirements are not detailed in this notice.

original_title: DPS 2 - Supply of community equipment paediatric and young adults
english_summary: London Borough of Croydon is establishing a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for the supply of paediatric and young adult community equipment on behalf of itself and partner local authorities through the Integrated Procurement Hub. This is DPS_2 consisting of 28 categories of paediatric community equipment, operating alongside an existing DPS for complex equipment.
what_they_want: 28 categories of paediatric and young adult community equipment (specific categories and models not detailed in this notice)
eligibility_requirements: Suppliers must meet DPS admission criteria set out in the Pre-Qualification Questionnaire (PQQ). Unsuccessful suppliers may re-submit revised PQQs at any time. The DPS remains open to new suppliers throughout its validity period.

original_title: Cardiac CT and MR Diagnostic Imaging Services
english_summary: Manchester University NHS Foundation Trust is seeking a supplier to provide cardiac diagnostic imaging services using CT and MR technology to supplement their existing in-house capabilities at their Oxford Road location.
what_they_want: Cardiac CT and MR diagnostic imaging services to supplement existing in-house provision at Oxford Road site

original_title: GB-Middlesbrough: 25.66
english_summary: Teesside University is procuring equipment for treating dry eye disease to be used in their specialist clinic and for student teaching purposes. This is an initial equipment purchase tender.
what_they_want: Equipment for the treatment of dry eye disease (specific models and brands not mentioned in the notice)

original_title: Occupational Health and Related Services - DPS
english_summary: Crown Commercial Service is establishing a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for occupational health and related services for UK public sector, wider public sector, and third sector organizations. The procurement covers occupational health services, employee assistance programmes, fully managed services, eyecare services, and hearing services under the Light Touch Regime.
what_they_want: Occupational health services, employee assistance programmes, fully managed service, eyecare services, and hearing services.

original_title: Health Checks DPS
english_summary: West Northamptonshire Council is procuring providers to deliver NHS Health Checks for people aged 40-74 to identify and manage cardiovascular risk factors. The contract runs from April 2024 to February 2029 and aims to deliver approximately 27,417-34,650 health checks annually to eligible residents.
what_they_want: NHS Health Check services for cardiovascular disease risk assessment and management for eligible population aged 40-74 years with no previous history of CVD. Services must comply with NHS Health Check programme requirements under the Health and Social Care Act 2012.

original_title: Provision of Best Interest Assessors / Section 12 Doctors - Pseudo Open Framework
english_summary: Hull City Council is tendering for a pseudo open framework agreement to provide Best Interest Assessors and Section 12 Doctors under the Light Touch Regime. The framework will run for 3 years from April 2025 to March 2028 with an optional 24-month extension, with an estimated value of £200,000 per annum.
what_they_want: Provision of Best Interest Assessors (Lot 1) and Section 12 Doctors (Lot 2). Direct awards will be administered via rotation from the list of suppliers in each lot.

original_title: Functional Electrical Stimulation Supplies and Maintenance
buyer_name: Procurement Assist Limited c/o Integrated Facilities Management Bolton Ltd (iFM Bolton)
english_summary: This is a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for the supply of Functional Electrical Stimulation (FES) supplies and maintenance to UK public healthcare providers and other contracting authorities. The DPS has been extended to run until 31 May 2029, with a total duration of 60 months from the amendment date.
what_they_want: Functional Electrical Stimulation (FES) supplies and maintenance services for use in general rehabilitation to complement therapy interventions.

original_title: ASC23043 - Positive Behaviour Support Supported Living Services in Herefordshire & Worcestershire DPS
english_summary: This is a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for Positive Behaviour Support (PBS) Supported Living Services in Herefordshire and Worcestershire, jointly established by Worcestershire County Council and NHS Worcestershire and Herefordshire Integrated Care Board. Suppliers can apply at any time throughout the DPS term to be pre-qualified for future service opportunities.
what_they_want: NOT FOUND
eligibility_requirements: Suitably experienced, qualified and appropriately accredited suppliers who can meet selection criteria and minimum standards as requested in the SSQ Questionnaire (Stage 1) and Evaluation (Stage 2). No maximum number of suppliers.

original_title: National Dynamic Purchasing System for Mobile Diagnostic and Treatment Units
english_summary: A Dynamic Purchasing System for mobile diagnostic and treatment units to increase NHS capacity for elective procedures and reduce waiting lists. Units will be deployed on hospital sites or in community locations and provided as vans or non-permanent cabins across three procurement options: hire/lease, purchase, or managed service.
what_they_want: Mobile diagnostic and/or treatment units in the form of vans or non-permanent units/cabins for healthcare delivery. Three lots available: Lot 1 - Hire or Lease of units; Lot 2 - Purchase of units; Lot 3 - Units as a Managed Service.

original_title: P0819A - Application to join the Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for the Domestic Homicide Reporting
english_summary: Birmingham City Council is establishing a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) to create a pool of Independent Authors/Chairs who can conduct statutory Domestic Homicide Reviews across the West Midlands region. These reviews examine deaths of persons aged 16+ resulting from domestic violence, abuse or neglect to identify lessons learned and prevent future incidents.
what_they_want: Independent Author/Chair services (combined role) to undertake statutory Domestic Homicide Reviews for deaths resulting from domestic violence, abuse or neglect across 7 Local Authorities in the West Midlands (Birmingham, Coventry, Dudley, Sandwell, Solihull, Walsall, and Wolverhampton).

original_title: ASC23034 Dynamic Purchasing System for the Provision of Older People Residential and Nursing Care Long Term and Replacement Care Services
english_summary: Worcestershire County Council is establishing a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for the provision of residential and nursing care services for older people across three lots covering long-term care, short-term replacement care, and emergency short-term replacement care. The DPS will run for four years with options to extend up to eight years total, with an estimated lifetime value of £1.1 billion excluding VAT.
what_they_want: NOT FOUND
eligibility_requirements: Suitably experienced, qualified and appropriately accredited suppliers (specific certification or experience requirements not detailed in the notice)

original_title: Nursing Homes Preferred Provider List For Derby and Derbyshire ICB
buyer_name: NHS Derby & Derbyshire Integrated Care Board
english_summary: NHS Derby & Derbyshire Integrated Care Board is establishing a Preferred Provider List for nursing home services using an Any Qualified Provider (AQP) basis, similar to a Dynamic Purchasing System. The framework will run for 5 years with an optional 2-year extension, allowing providers to apply at any time throughout the contract period.
what_they_want: Nursing home services for Derby and Derbyshire area residents. This is a zero value, zero volume framework contract for qualified nursing home providers.

original_title: Nursing Homes Preferred Provider List For Derby and Derbyshire ICB
english_summary: NHS Derby & Derbyshire ICB is establishing a Nursing Home Preferred Provider List on an Any Qualified Provider (AQP) basis, operating similarly to a Dynamic Purchasing System. The contract runs for 5 years with an optional 2-year extension, allowing providers to apply at any time during the contract period.
what_they_want: Nursing home services for placement on a preferred provider list. This is a zero value, zero volume framework agreement where individual contracts will be awarded as needed throughout the contract period.

original_title: Adults with Disabilities Pseudo-DPS
english_summary: This is a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for providing support services to adults with disabilities and long-term health conditions in Sheffield. The services include support within the home and activities outside the home for people aged 16 and over, running from March 2023 to March 2033.
what_they_want: Support services for adults with disabilities and/or long-term health conditions, including in-home support and activities/support outside the home for working-age adults (16+) living within Sheffield boundary.

original_title: Children and Young People Continuing Healthcare
buyer_name: NHS Arden and Greater East Midlands Commissioning Support Unit
english_summary: This is a tender for continuing healthcare services for children and young people with determined continuing care needs in Norfolk and Waveney. The service must be available 24/7, 365 days per year, and will be awarded as a Preferred Provider List on a zero value, zero volume basis for 3 years.
what_they_want: Continuing healthcare services for children and young people with continuing care needs, provided 24 hours a day, 365 days per year in accordance with service specifications and individual care plans.

original_title: Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for the supply of antibiotics and IV fluids
english_summary: This is a 10-year Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for procuring and stockpiling antibiotics and IV fluids to treat influenza-related secondary bacterial infections. The system will maintain a stockpile of 8-10 million treatment courses annually, with volumes subject to adjustment based on clinical guidance and population changes.
what_they_want: Antibiotics and IV fluids for treatment of influenza-related secondary bacterial infections. Suppliers may bid for multiple product types from the range specified.

original_title: Pseudo Dynamic Purchasing System (PDPS) for Rapid Specialist Opinion (RSO) / Referral Management Services (RMS)
buyer_name: NHS NORTH OF ENGLAND COMMISSIONING SUPPORT (HOSTED BY NHS ENGLAND)
english_summary: This tender establishes a Pseudo Dynamic Purchasing System (PDPS) for Rapid Specialist Opinion and Referral Management Services on behalf of multiple NHS Clinical Commissioning Groups in North East England. The system will allow participating NHS organizations to procure specialist medical opinion and patient referral management services.
what_they_want: Rapid Specialist Opinion (RSO) services and Referral Management Services (RMS) for patient care coordination and specialist medical consultations.

original_title: Flexible Procurement System (FPS) for the Provision of Independent Assessors and Reviewers
english_summary: City of Doncaster Council is establishing a Flexible Procurement System for the provision of independent assessors and reviewers across seven lots covering mental health assessments, safeguarding reviews, and complaints handling. This is not a procurement for medical devices but rather for professional assessment and review services.
what_they_want: NOT FOUND

original_title: Older Peoples Care Homes
english_summary: This is a procurement for care home services for older people in Sheffield, focused on delivering personalized care that promotes independence, wellbeing, and quality of life while supporting the city's Health and Social Care vision to keep people well at home and reduce hospital admissions.
what_they_want: NOT FOUND
